finding.message: Unsafe deserialization depends on a [user-provided value](1).

verification.reasoning: User-controlled input flows directly to `pickle.loads()` without adequate sanitization or defense. Although a default value is assigned when `raw` is falsy, this does not prevent exploitation when user input produces a truthy `raw`. The function appears to be self-contained and directly processes HTTP request data.
verification.data_flow: request.body or request.GET.get("blob") (line 65) → raw (line 65) → base64.b64decode(raw) (line 67) → raw (line 67) → pickle.loads(raw) (line 70)
answers: The EXACT deserialization sink is `pickle.loads(raw)` on line 70. The bytes value passed to it is `raw`.

The assignments to `raw` are: line 65: `raw = request.body or request.GET.get("blob", "").encode("utf-8")`, line 67: `raw = base64.b64decode(raw)`, and line 69: `raw = b"\x80\x04}q\x00."` (conditional). The last assignment on the path where `raw` reaches the sink is line 67 or 69, depending on conditions.

The value of `raw` derives from user input: `request.body` (line 65) or `request.GET.get("blob", "")` (line 65), which are HTTP request components. It may be transformed by base64 decoding (line 67) or replaced by a constant (line 69).
Yes, there is a constant-time condition on line 69: `if not raw: raw = b"\x80\x04}q\x00."`. This overrides the user-tainted value if `raw` is falsy after line 67.

There is no specific defense preventing code execution. The code uses `pickle.loads()` directly on data that can be user-controlled. The conditional override on line 69 sets a default constant, but this does not sanitize user-provided paths that result in a truthy `raw`.

The data flow shows user input (`request.body` or `request.GET["blob"]`) reaching `pickle.loads()` via `raw`. There is no visible defense like `yaml.safe_load()`, HMAC verification, or schema validation. The conditional assignment on line 69 is not sufficient to prevent all user-controlled paths from reaching the sink.
